edges.description: Fistula-associated mucinous tumors may infiltrate adjacent pelvic structures.
Fistula-associated mucinous anal adenocarcinoma can present with progressive anal pain and a palpable tumor.
Primary anal adenocarcinoma can present with rectal bleeding or serosanguineous anal discharge that prompts anorectal evaluation.
edges.target: Rectal bleeding or serosanguineous anal discharge

edges.description: Locally advanced anorectal fistula cancer can cause anal stenosis through a pelvic mass.
The anal gland/transitional subtype shows greater immune checkpoint expression.
edges.source: Dualistic HPV-dependent and HPV-independent tumor lineages

edges.description: Targetable gastrointestinal cancer genes vary across subtypes.
edges.source: Dualistic HPV-dependent and HPV-independent tumor lineages

nodes.description: Anal or perianal pain with a palpable tumor can be a presenting feature, especially in fistula-associated mucinous adenocarcinoma.

evidence.reference_title: A case report of anal fistula-associated mucinous adenocarcinoma developing 3 years after treatment of perianal abscess.
evidence.snippet: A 68-year-old woman was admitted to our hospital because of progressive anal pain and a palpable tumor.

nodes.description: Locally advanced anorectal fistula cancer can present with anal stenosis caused by a pelvic mass and may be associated with abdominal pain and diarrhea in Crohn disease.

evidence.reference_title: Resection of anorectal fistula cancer associated with Crohn's disease after preoperative chemoradiotherapy: a case report.
evidence.snippet: Computed tomography (CT) showed anal stenosis due to a pelvic mass.

nodes.description: Recurrent perianal abscess and anal fistula generate a chronic inflammatory epithelial tract. Dysplasia within the fistula lining can progress to poorly differentiated mucinous adenocarcinoma involving the dermis, epidermis, sphincter complex, or adjacent pelvic organs.

evidence.reference_title: A case report of anal fistula-associated mucinous adenocarcinoma developing 3 years after treatment of perianal abscess.
evidence.snippet: Histopathology showed highly dysplastic cells lining the lumen of the anal fistula and poorly differentiated mucinous adenocarcinoma proliferating in the dermis and epidermis in the distal aspect of the fistula.

nodes.description: Primary anal canal adenocarcinoma separates into at least two region-specific entities. Anal gland/transitional-type tumors can follow an HPV-dependent pathway with increased immune checkpoint expression, whereas colorectal-type tumors more often follow HPV-independent colorectal-lineage molecular programs.
nodes.id: Dualistic HPV-dependent and HPV-independent tumor lineages

biological_processes: cell surface receptor protein tyrosine kinase signaling pathway
cell_types: glandular epithelial cell of anal canal

evidence.reference_title: A dualistic model of primary anal canal adenocarcinoma with distinct cellular origins, etiologies, inflammatory microenvironments and mutational signatures: implications for personalised medicine.
evidence.snippet: The significant differences in the HPV status, density of tumour-infiltrating lymphocytes, expression of immune checkpoints and mutational profile of several targetable genes further supported the separation of these latter neoplasms into two distinct entities.

nodes.description: The anal gland/transitional-type subset has higher PD-1/PD-L1 expression and a different tumor-infiltrating lymphocyte profile than colorectal-type tumors, creating a biologic rationale for immune-checkpoint-directed clinical research while not establishing prospective efficacy for this rare tumor.

evidence.reference_title: A dualistic model of primary anal canal adenocarcinoma with distinct cellular origins, etiologies, inflammatory microenvironments and mutational signatures: implications for personalised medicine.
evidence.snippet: anal gland/transitional-type cancers, which poorly respond to standard treatments, displayed less mutations in downstream effectors of the EGFR signalling pathway (i.e., KRAS and NRAS) and demonstrated a significantly higher expression of the immune inhibitory ligand-receptor pair PD-1/PD-L1 compared to their counterparts arising from the colorectal mucosa.

nodes.description: Bleeding or serosanguineous drainage may prompt anorectal examination even when prior colorectal screening was normal.
nodes.id: Rectal bleeding or serosanguineous anal discharge

evidence.reference_title: Anal adenocarcinoma: case report, literature review and comparative survival analysis.
evidence.snippet: This case highlights the challenges of early diagnosis of anal malignancies and the importance of prompt evaluation of anorectal area by anoscopy and colonoscopy if symptoms of rectal bleeding or serosanguinous anal discharge are reported.
